Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»BREAKING: Team South Africa handed 4x400m relay lifeline

Team South Africa’s men’s 4x400m relay team were handed a lifeline after finishing last in their semi-final at the Paris Olympics on Friday.

Team South Africa’smen’s4x400mrelay team were handed a lifeline after finishing last in their semi-final at the Paris Olympics on Friday.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

The quartet of Gardeo Isaacs, Zakhiti Nene, Antonie Nortje and Lythe Pillay finished eighth in their heat in 3:03.19 and didn’t qualify for the final.

Nortje ran into trouble at the changeover when he collided with the Spanish runner in front of him and fell.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

However, in post race drama,Nigeriawere disqualified and Team South Africa were somewhat puzzlingly promoted to the final by the referee, despite not finishing with the next fastest time.

No fault – nor disqualification – was handed out to any other team involved in Nortje’s fall.

Nortje grabbed the back of the Spanish runner, slowing him down as he fell.

Spain were neither disqualified nor promoted to the final – despite finishing ahead of South Africa.

Wayde van Niekerkcould be drafted into the South Africa line-up for the final which is scheduled to take place onSaturday, 10 Augustat21:00(SA time).

title: BREAKING: Team South Africa handed 4x400m relay lifeline
author: Garrin Lambley
description: Team South Africa's men's 4x400m relay team were handed a lifeline after finishing last in their semi-final at the Paris Olympics on Friday.
